will give the Moon's position at conception.
(6) The Rasi occupied by the Moon at birth will be rising
or setting at conception according to as the Moon is waxing
or waning.
Now let us work out an example according to these princi-
ples There was a birth at 5.58 A.M., LS.T. on 1952-7-6,
Lat. 12.5 N. The positions of the Moon, Sun and Ascendant
aregiven below : —Moon = 7tl-29'-40'-21'. Sun = 2a-20o-40'-43*,
Asc. ™2M70-l'-32*. See the chart on page 178. Here the
Moon is waxing and invisible. Hence the interval between
conception and birth is greater than 10 lunar revolutions. As
the Moon is invisible we have to deduct the ascendant from the
Moon's position :—78-29"-40'-2* minus 2"-17n-l'-32' - 54-12',-38'-
49"= 162.647 degrees. On dividing this by 12, we get 13.554.
This is the number of days we have to add to 273.2305. By
addition we get 286.7845. This is the period preceding birth
or the interval between conception and birth. As it is bright
